nodes.label: AI as inspiration source for coding
nodes.grounding_span: If I want a quick double check to make sure there are no typos or logic errors, AI can be great for that

nodes.label: AI code correctness and maintainability
nodes.grounding_span: all AI code needs to be double checked

nodes.label: AI context awareness and up-to-date information
nodes.grounding_span: the bot typically won't have access to source code or documentation for dependencies

nodes.label: AI autocomplete and refactoring tools
nodes.grounding_span: I ended up turning them off

nodes.label: AI as productivity booster with quality control
nodes.grounding_span: emphasize using AI to boost productivity, but be very strict about professionalism quality and double checking everything

nodes.label: Personal skill development and learning
nodes.grounding_span: a desire to reinforce my own skill set

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for research and inspiration
nodes.grounding_span: I've been incredibly satisfied because a chatbot suggested an API that I didn't know existed

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucinations and context loss
nodes.grounding_span: hallucinations, long conversations with long context, or the AI just following what I'm saying (even when I'm incorrect) - have been frustrating

nodes.label: Openness to future AI integration
nodes.grounding_span: I'd like to use AI to automate things where I can

nodes.label: Preference for manual coding over autocomplete
nodes.grounding_span: I ended up turning them off

nodes.label: Iterative context resetting — opens fresh chat when context overload
nodes.grounding_span: I'll literally open a fresh chat and put the relevant info up front

nodes.label: Deliberate skill reinforcement through manual coding
nodes.grounding_span: a desire to reinforce my own skill set. Literally typing the code myself helps me learn
